learningObjective: Discover that limits can exist even when the function is undefined at that point
ahaMoment: The limit exists at y=4 even though there's a hole in the function at x=2

miniSpec.title: Fill the Hole Game
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: Point moves vertically, color changes to green when correctly placed
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: Drag point → y-value updates → when y=4 the curve completes → limit discovered
miniSpec.aha_moment: Placing the point at y=4 fills the hole perfectly, revealing the limit

modelVariables.holeFillerY: y-position of the draggable filler point (0-6)
modelVariables.holeFilledCorrectly: derived: boolean whether the point is within tolerance of the correct limit

assertions.description: On load, the filler point is NOT at the correct position, hole is not filled
assertions.visualCheck: Red point is visible but not at y=4, hole in the curve is obvious

assertions.description: Dragging the point updates the y-position
assertions.visualCheck: Point moves to y=3, still red, hole not filled

assertions.description: Placing the point at y=4 fills the hole and triggers success
assertions.visualCheck: Point turns green, positioned at the hole, success message appears
